title: Isomerism
summary: 9701 AS isomerism — structural, E/Z, and optical isomerism with chiral centres.
learningObjectives: Define structural isomerism and draw chain, position, and functional group isomers.
Explain E/Z isomerism for restricted rotation about C=C.
Identify chiral centres and optical isomerism.
State why enantiomers rotate plane-polarised light in opposite directions.

simpleExplanation.title: Same formula, different arrangement
simpleExplanation.summary: Isomers share molecular formula but differ in structure or spatial arrangement. Stereoisomers need identical connectivity but different 3D arrangement.
steps: Structural: different connectivity (chain, position, functional group).
E/Z: different groups on each end of C=C — need Cahn-Ingold-Prelog priority rules.
Chiral centre: carbon with four different groups → non-superimposable mirror images.
Racemic mixture: equal enantiomers → no net optical activity.

sections.content: **Isomerism** (13.4) appears in almost every organic exam — draw **all structural isomers** of C₄H₁₀O or identify **chiral centres** in a given structure.
For E/Z naming: assign priority by atomic number at first point of difference. If higher priority groups are on **same side** → Z (zusammen); **opposite** → E (entgegen).
